title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 18)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: An Abington one-family ranch on Rice St., 704 sq. ft., sold for $460,000.
A Boston condo on Beacon St., 5,703 sq. ft., sold for $17,000,000.
An Arlington one-family Colonial on Lincoln St., 3,292 sq. ft., sold for $2,500,000.

title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' power play efficiency dropped from 26.3% to 12.8%, ranking 28th in the league recently.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have failed to score at 5-on-5 play post-Olympics, impacting the Bruins' depth scoring.
The third defensive pair struggles with Andrew Peeke frequently swapped with Henri Jokiharju, both showing limited effectiveness.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Venezuela wins its first World Baseball Classic title, defeating Team USA 3-2, with key contributions from Wilyer Abreu.
Red Sox outfielder Wilyer Abreu hit two significant home runs for Venezuela, including one in the championship game.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez delivered over four scoreless innings, helping Venezuela secure the title.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center supports children with complex medical needs and provides relief for their families.
Flanagan, a parent of two, has volunteered at the center and is raising funds to assist these families.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker were found guilty of involuntary manslaughter and reckless child endangerment in Berkshire County.
The couple ignored signs the child needed medical care, leading to death from a treatable strep infection and pneumonia.
They face up to 20 years for manslaughter and up to 2.5 years for child endangerment, with sentencing on March 19.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A water main break flooded Jackson Square, affecting Columbus Avenue and Centre and Ritchie streets in Jamaica Plain.
Police rerouted traffic and the MBTA detoured several bus routes due to street flooding in the area.
Crews are still working to shut off water; three daycare businesses evacuated and remain closed.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Waddle was traded from the Miami Dolphins to the Denver Broncos for three picks including a 2026 first-rounder.
The trade improves the Broncos, a top AFC contender, potentially complicating the Patriots' prospects for next season.
The Patriots may need a first-round pick to acquire A.J. Brown, following the trade's impact on receiver market value.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce, 45, and Maria Moraes, 25, charged after allegedly hitting three pedestrians with a Jeep Wrangler outside ICON nightclub.
Boyce faces multiple charges including felony assault, firearm storage violation, reckless driving; Moraes charged with police interference, disorderly conduct.
Incident involved an unidentified man with a gun; victims identified Boyce and Moraes from a 'show-up identification', police found a firearm in the Jeep.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made an unexpected appearance at the Irish American Partnership's St. Patrick’s Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden's speech focused on Irish ancestry and immigrant experiences, referencing his family's history and current immigration challenges.
Governor Maura Healey spoke on immigrant treatment, emphasizing Massachusetts' connection to Irish American heritage and calling for reflection.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students injured after a deck railing collapse during a party at 64 Undine Road, Brighton.
Police found rotting wood and corroded nails on the deck, potentially concealed by paint, at the incident site.
Victims were taken to local hospitals; the house is under safety assessment and the incident is under investigation.
